diagnostics.message: "scroll" listener without { passive: true } — blocks scrolling performance. Only add { passive: true } if the handler does NOT call event.preventDefault() (passive listeners silently ignore preventDefault())
diagnostics.help: Add `{ passive: true }` as the third argument: `addEventListener('scroll', handler, { passive: true })`. Only do this if the handler does NOT call `event.preventDefault()` — passive listeners silently ignore `preventDefault()`, which breaks features like pull-to-refresh suppression, custom gestures, and nested-scroll containment.

diagnostics.message: "scroll" listener without { passive: true } — blocks scrolling performance. Only add { passive: true } if the handler does NOT call event.preventDefault() (passive listeners silently ignore preventDefault())
diagnostics.help: Add `{ passive: true }` as the third argument: `addEventListener('scroll', handler, { passive: true })`. Only do this if the handler does NOT call `event.preventDefault()` — passive listeners silently ignore `preventDefault()`, which breaks features like pull-to-refresh suppression, custom gestures, and nested-scroll containment.

diagnostics.message: useSearchParams() requires a <Suspense> boundary — without one, the entire page bails out to client-side rendering
diagnostics.help: Wrap the component using useSearchParams: `<Suspense fallback={<Skeleton />}><SearchComponent /></Suspense>`
